name: Red Sea / Suez Canal
summary: DAY 35 ZERO SHIPPING ATTACKS. Effective closure via carrier withdrawal (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC rerouting). Iran pushing Houthis to 'prepare' (Bloomberg) but leadership divisions persist. Non-attack for 35 days = either strategic reserve or real internal divisions. MARAD advisory active, ASPIDES ready.

signals.content: Houthis entered the war with a missile at Israel on March 28 but have NOT attacked Red Sea shipping (yet). UKMTO and maritime trackers report no new incidents in the Red Sea corridor. However, the Houthi crossing from rhetoric to action materially increases the probability of Red Sea shipping attacks. Michael Knights (Washington Institute) expects incremental escalation: missile at Israel → Red Sea threat → actual shipping attack, spaced 5-10 days apart. Container freight up 3rd consecutive week anticipating disruption. (Sources: UKMTO, IDF, Al Jazeera, Drewry, Maritime Hub)

Day 33: STILL zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping. Attacks remain Israel-targeting only (2 strikes Mar 28-29). However, Bab al-Mandeb is in EFFECTIVE commercial closure due to carrier voluntary withdrawal — Maersk rerouted ME11/MECL to Cape of Good Hope, CMA CGM/Hapag-Lloyd/MSC also rerouted. Bloomberg reports 'divisions within Houthi leadership' partially explaining delay. Houthis told Lloyd's List they see 'no reason to prevent' Yanbu VLCC trade 'at present' — preserving shipping card as leverage. Houthi deputy minister: 'closing Bab al-Mandeb is among our options.' IRGC Tasnim warned of 'new fronts at Red Sea' if ground ops on Iranian islands. EU Operation ASPIDES extended to Feb 2027. UKMTO threat level: CRITICAL. The pattern: threat is generating commercial cost (rerouting, insurance) without expenditure — the threat IS the weapon. (Sources: UKMTO, MARAD, gCaptain, Bloomberg, Lloyd's List, France 24)

Day 35: Still zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping. Pattern holds: rhetoric without maritime action. EU Operation ASPIDES ready for resumed attacks. MARAD Advisory 2026-006 remains active. Effective commercial closure via carrier voluntary withdrawal (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC rerouting to Cape). Bloomberg reports Houthi leadership divisions. Iran pushing Houthis to 'prepare for renewed campaign' per European officials (Bloomberg). The non-attack is intelligence: 35 days of threats without shipping action suggests Houthis are being held as a strategic reserve or internal divisions are real. (Sources: MARAD, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, France 24)

edges.context: Houthis control Yemen's Red Sea coastline and have demonstrated capability to attack shipping (2024-25 campaign). Currently in the war but haven't attacked shipping yet.
~12% of global trade transits Red Sea/Suez. If Houthis attack shipping, route diversions to Cape of Good Hope add 10-14 days and massive cost.
If BOTH Hormuz and Red Sea are disrupted simultaneously, there is no safe Gulf export route. This is the worst-case scenario for global energy trade.
Saudi Arabia's Yanbu pipeline exports via Red Sea bypass Hormuz. If Red Sea is also disrupted, this bypass becomes useless. Oil to $130+.

trigger_points.condition: Houthis attack commercial shipping in the Red Sea
trigger_points.mechanism: Double chokepoint activated. Cape of Good Hope only route. Shipping rates double. Oil $130+. India acute energy crisis. Container freight spikes. Global trade disrupted.
